name: edgee
install_doc: README: `curl -fsSL https://edgee.ai/install.sh | bash` then `edgee launch claude` (routes Claude Code through the Edgee gateway). THOL pins the v0.2.12 release binary (sha256-verified, x86_64-linux-gnu asset). DOCUMENTED DEVIATION: `edgee launch` requires an Edgee ACCOUNT (interactive browser OAuth; adds cloud analytics/session history) — not reproducible headless. THOL instead runs the auth-free `edgee local-gateway` per run on a dedicated ${PORT} and points the session at it via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL; per its own help this serves 'POST /v1/messages -> Anthropic Messages API (passthrough + compression)' — the same Edgee compression pipeline, minus the cloud account features.
notes: Rust LLM gateway ('Same output, fewer tokens, lower bill'): compresses redundant tool output (logs, file listings, build results) in the request context before it reaches the model. Derived from RTK (credited upstream) but proxy-based instead of hook-based, so it needs no agent opt-in — adoption is N/A like headroom/rtk. Validated on this machine 2026-07-10: full headless Claude Code session through the local gateway (correct answer, cost recorded, gateway stable, teardown clean).
setup_commands: nohup edgee local-gateway --port ${PORT} >"$HOME/edgee-gateway.log" 2>&1 & for i in $(seq 40); do curl -s -o /dev/null "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/" && exit 0; sleep 0.25; done; echo 'edgee local-gateway did not come up' >&2; exit 1
